rationale: In the citing paper, Nunes (2000) appears only in a grouped citation used to document prior evidence of tariff-choice biases, namely that customers may pick higher-allowance plans even when bills are higher. The authors do not engage Nunes' cognitive mechanism, variables, or empirical design in a paper-specific way, nor do they derive their model structure from it. The citation serves background positioning in a literature list rather than a distinct functional building block.
